terms.definition: The customs, traditions, language, food, clothing, festivals, and way of life shared by a group of people.
terms.exampleUsage: Every state in India has its own unique culture.

terms.definition: The presence of many different types of people, cultures, languages, and traditions in one place.
terms.exampleUsage: India's diversity can be seen in its many languages and festivals.

terms.definition: A belief, custom, or way of doing something that has been passed down from parents and grandparents over many years.
terms.exampleUsage: It is a tradition in many families to light a diya during Diwali.

terms.definition: A special time of celebration marked by activities like prayers, feasts, music, dance, and gatherings.
terms.exampleUsage: Holi is a festival of colours celebrated with great joy across India.

terms.definition: A group of people who live together or share common interests, beliefs, or backgrounds.
terms.exampleUsage: People in our community come together to celebrate Republic Day.

terms.definition: The valuable things like buildings, art, traditions, and practices that are passed down from earlier generations.
terms.exampleUsage: The Taj Mahal is part of India's rich cultural heritage.

terms.definition: The first language a child learns at home from their parents and family.
terms.exampleUsage: My mother tongue is Marathi, but I also speak Hindi and English at school.

terms.definition: Including everyone and making sure no one is left out because of their differences.
terms.exampleUsage: Our school practises inclusivity by welcoming children from all backgrounds.

terms.definition: A regular practice or way of behaving that is special to a particular group or community.
terms.exampleUsage: It is a custom to touch the feet of elders as a sign of respect in many Indian families.

terms.definition: The idea that people with different cultures, languages, and religions can still live together in peace and harmony as one nation.
terms.exampleUsage: India shows unity in diversity when people of all religions celebrate each other's festivals.
